skill.name: imagegen-frontend-web
skill.description: Generates art-directed frontend design references as separate horizontal images for website sections, with consistent palette, varied layouts, strong imagery, and implementation-focused visual guidance.
skill.summary: Create premium, image-led website section references for frontend builds.
skill.author: leonxlnx

security_audit.summary: The static backtick findings at SKILL.md lines 81-95 are Markdown inline text for design scale explanations, not shell or Ruby execution. The weak cryptography and reconnaissance alerts correspond to natural-language design guidance and example prompts in SKILL.md, with no executable code, network calls, credential handling, or prompt-injection attempt found.

content.value_statement: Teams need website concepts that look premium and are clear enough to implement. This skill directs Claude, Codex, or Claude Code to create consistent image-led section references for landing pages and product sites.
actual_capabilities: Generates one separate horizontal image direction for every requested website section.
Defaults unspecified landing pages to six sections and full website templates to eight sections.
Guides hero composition away from repetitive left-text and right-image layouts.
Maintains a consistent palette, typography direction, and visual system across all sections.
Defines rules for hero scale, spacing, imagery, CTAs, and section rhythm.
Uses clarity checks so visual references remain readable and implementation-friendly.
limitations: It produces visual references and image prompts, not production frontend code.
Final image quality depends on the connected image generation model.
It does not verify brand asset licenses or real product imagery rights.
Exact copy, compliance claims, and accessibility details may need human review.

use_cases.title: Landing Page Concepts
use_cases.description: Create a multi-section campaign page with varied composition, clear CTAs, and one consistent visual system.

use_cases.title: Product Site Direction
use_cases.description: Turn a product brief into premium website section references before hiring a designer or frontend engineer.

use_cases.title: Implementation References
use_cases.description: Generate section images that clarify layout, spacing, hierarchy, and component styling for a build.

prompt_templates.title: Beginner Landing Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when you need a complete first pass from a short brief.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a six-section landing page concept for a B2B analytics product. Make one separate horizontal image per section, with a consistent palette and varied layouts.

prompt_templates.title: Style-Guided Product Page
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when brand mood and conversion goals are known.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create eight horizontal section references for a premium product website. Use editorial imagery, restrained CTAs, generous spacing, and a clear proof-to-action flow.

prompt_templates.title: Engineer-Ready Reference Set
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when images must be easy to translate into frontend components.
prompt_templates.prompt: Design separate website section images that clearly show grid structure, spacing, typography scale, CTA priority, image treatment, and responsive intent.

prompt_templates.title: Advanced Art Direction System
prompt_templates.scenario: Use when a team needs a distinctive creative direction across many sections.
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a twelve-section creative agency website reference set. Vary composition anchors, background modes, hero scale, visual density, and section rhythm while keeping one coherent design system.

output_examples.input: Design a six-section landing page for a climate analytics SaaS.
output: Six separate horizontal section images are planned: hero, proof, product story, use cases, pricing, and final CTA.
The references use a consistent ink, paper, and green accent palette with varied image crops and clear conversion flow.

output_examples.input: Create a hero section for a premium AI startup.
output_examples.output: One horizontal hero reference is generated with a non-default composition, strong visual hierarchy, a clear CTA, and restrained image-led atmosphere.

output_examples.input: Make twelve sections for a creative studio website.
output: Twelve separate section images are produced instead of one tall mockup.
The set uses off-grid editorial layouts, controlled palette reuse, and generous spacing so developers can recreate each section.

best_practices: State the site type, audience, section count, brand mood, and primary conversion goal.
Provide any required palette, copy, product visuals, or accessibility constraints before generation.
Review each section as an implementation reference, not only as a mood image.
anti_patterns: Do not request one tall image when each website section needs a separate reference.
Do not use vague prompts that omit audience, product category, or conversion goal.
Do not treat generated copy, brand claims, or image assets as legally approved content.

faq.question: Does this skill generate frontend code?
faq.answer: No. It generates image directions and section references that help a developer or coding model build the page.

faq.question: How many images does it create for a landing page?
faq.answer: If no count is provided, it defaults to six separate horizontal section images.

faq.question: Can it support a full website template?
faq.answer: Yes. It defaults a full website template to eight separate horizontal section references.

faq.question: What makes the output different from generic AI website art?
faq.answer: It emphasizes composition variety, consistent palettes, readable hierarchy, section rhythm, and implementation clarity.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish in the marketplace?
faq.answer: Yes. The reviewed file contains Markdown design instructions and no confirmed executable security risk.

faq.question: What should users provide for best results?
faq.answer: Users should provide the product type, audience, desired style, section count, conversion goal, and any brand constraints.
